stories.title: 5.6 million taxpayers check their pay in the HMRC app an average of 18 times a year
stories.summary: HMRC reports that 5.6 million taxpayers use the HMRC app regularly, averaging 18 checks per year. The announcement accompanies the publication of the HMRC Transformation Roadmap update.

stories.title: HMRC Transformation Roadmap: update 2026
stories.summary: HMRC published its 2026 Transformation Roadmap update, highlighting that over 7.6 million people used the HMRC app in 2025/26 (up from 5.9 million the year before) and £818.8 million in Self Assessment payments were made digitally. Plans include 4,000+ GOV.UK guidance updates and expanded fraud detection.

stories.title: No 10 shares 'public's shock' at reports of convicted people smuggler living in UK
stories.summary: Downing Street is urgently investigating a BBC report that the 'godfather' of Calais people smuggling networks — a convicted people smuggler — is living in the UK and has not been deported. The Home Office faces pressure to explain the situation.

stories.title: Police recorded knife-enabled robbery offences, year ending June 2026
stories.summary: The Home Office announced forthcoming management information on knife-enabled robberies recorded by police for the year ending June 2026, continuing its series of crime statistics publications.

stories.title: Police use of firearms statistics, England and Wales: April 2025 to March 2026
stories.summary: The Home Office released its annual firearms statistics for English and Welsh police forces, covering the full 2025/26 financial year with pre-release access now published.

stories.title: UK steps up support for Venezuela following devastating earthquakes
stories.summary: The UK is scaling up its humanitarian response to Venezuela following devastating earthquakes on 24 June, mobilising an additional £3.8 million in emergency aid. This brings total UK support to a significant level as the FCDO coordinates the international response.

stories.title: FCDO updates Nigeria travel advice: new warnings for Kwara and Benue States
stories.summary: The FCDO has updated its Nigeria travel advice, now advising against all but essential travel to Kwara State and Benue State in addition to existing advisories. Travellers are urged to review the latest guidance before visiting.

stories.title: UK-Mauritius Chagos Treaty: Pen for Rights seeks clarification from UK Foreign Secretary
stories.summary: Civil society group Pen for Rights has written to the UK Foreign Secretary seeking clarification on the diplomatic process behind the proposed UK-Mauritius Folhlhavai (Chagos) Treaty, raising questions about transparency and consultation.

stories.title: Get Britain Working: an update on the Jobs and Careers Service
stories.summary: DWP published an update on the new Jobs and Careers Service, outlining how specialised Jobcentre Plus support will focus on groups facing specific barriers to work, including disabled people and long-term sick individuals.

stories.title: Keep Britain Working review update: June 2026
stories.summary: DWP released the June 2026 update on implementing the independent Keep Britain Working review, which examines employers' role in tackling health-based economic inactivity. The update addresses the urgent crisis of people leaving the workforce due to ill-health.

agencies.name: DEFRA
agencies.description: Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs

stories.title: Almost 20,000 new homes unlocked after breakthrough by Government Water Delivery Taskforce
stories.summary: DEFRA and Housing Minister Emma Reynolds announced that over 18,000 new homes have been unlocked following a breakthrough by the Government's Water Delivery Taskforce, resolving water infrastructure constraints that had blocked major housing developments.

stories.title: Annual UK air pollutant concentration data published
stories.summary: DEFRA published its annual update of data on concentrations of major air pollutants across the UK, providing the latest environmental monitoring figures for public and policy use.

stories.title: Kent opens first VALOUR-recognised centre in new UK-wide veteran support system
stories.summary: Thousands of veterans in Kent can now access increased support following the opening of a new VALOUR-recognised support centre in Aylesford on 2 July. The centre is part of a new UK-wide framework for veteran welfare.

stories.title: UK armed forces mental health 2025/26: annual report published
stories.summary: The MoD's annual mental health report for UK armed forces shows that 11.8% of personnel were seen by military healthcare services for a mental health-related reason in 2025/26 — a statistically significant rate, underscoring ongoing investment in mental health support.

stories.title: War Pensions Scheme Statistics: 2026
stories.summary: The Ministry of Defence published its 2026 War Pensions Scheme statistics, covering recipients of war pensions, new claims, awards, and appeals under the scheme.

stories.title: Trump warns UK against deepening business ties with China
stories.summary: President Trump warned that it is 'very dangerous' for the UK to deepen business ties with China, just as Prime Minister Keir Starmer completed talks with Chinese counterparts. The statement puts DBT's trade strategy in the international spotlight.

agencies.name: DSIT
agencies.description: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

stories.title: Responsible AI Through Cloud Technology: first joint government-academia event held
stories.summary: DSIT hosted the first joint Cloud, FinOps, and AI community event bringing together professionals from across government and academia to discuss responsible AI implementation in cloud technology environments.

stories.title: Women in Research Charter published
stories.summary: DSIT published the Women in Research Charter, a voluntary commitment designed to improve outcomes for women across the UK research system, addressing gender imbalances in academia and R&D.

stories.title: NHS maternity review published: new standards and commissioner proposed
stories.summary: A major NHS maternity review has been published, proposing new transparency standards, a new maternity commissioner, and greater accountability. The Guardian noted debate over whether the review goes far enough following high-profile maternity scandals.

stories.title: NHS England orders trust boards to enforce joint maternity accountability
stories.summary: The CEO of NHS England has ordered trust boards to enforce joint accountability for maternity services between medical directors and chief nursing officers, following ongoing scrutiny of maternity care standards across English NHS trusts.

stories.title: Former NHS assistant found guilty of £279k fraud
stories.summary: An NHS Counter Fraud Authority investigation led to the conviction of a former NHS employee who caused a £279,000 loss to Worcestershire NHS by stealing and misusing funds. The case highlights ongoing anti-fraud efforts within the NHS.

stories.title: Royal Mail confirms major changes to UK letter delivery targets
stories.summary: Royal Mail has confirmed significant changes to its delivery service: first-class next-day delivery targets drop from 93% to 90%, and second-class delivery standards are also revised. The changes represent the most significant restructuring of Royal Mail's service targets in years.

stories.title: Post Office Horizon quashed convictions update: 2 July 2026
stories.summary: The Government published the latest management information on progress in quashing convictions under the Post Office Horizon System Offences Act 2024, updating figures on how many wrongful convictions in England and Wales have been overturned.
